content.content:
{"body":"* **GO term ID and label for which you request a new superclass**

GO:0061852 retrograde transporter complex, Golgi to ER
Transporter complex that recognises, binds and returns endoplasmic reticulum (ER) resident proteins that have trafficked to Golgi compartments. Targets proteins lacking the HDEL motif recognised by COPI-coated vesicles. PMID:16093310

* **New superclass (parent) suggested**

GO:0062137  cargo receptor complex

* ** refine definiton **

Transporter complex that recognises => Cargo receptor complex that recognizes

* ** Change primary label **

to 
retrograde cargo receptor complex, Golgi to ER 

make GO:0061852 retrograde transporter complex, Golgi to ER | broad (this  label is not restricted to cargo, could include other retrograde complexes)

*— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-04-22T07:36:11Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31935#issuecomment-4294405363","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7_-CSj","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Done — PR #31946 opens the following changes on `GO:0061852`:

- **Primary label** is now *retrograde cargo receptor complex, Golgi to ER*.
- **Definition** refined: "Transporter complex that recognises…" → "Cargo receptor complex that recognizes…". The rest of the definition is unchanged.
- **Parent**: `is_a GO:1990351 ! transporter complex` replaced with `is_a GO:0062137 ! cargo receptor complex`. Rationale: `cargo receptor activity` (GO:0038024) explicitly contrasts with transmembrane transporter activity ("Cargo receptors transport substances by vesicular transport, not by transmembrane transport."), so this complex — which recognises ER-resident proteins lacking HDEL and returns them via vesicular transport — fits cleanly under `cargo receptor complex` and should no longer sit under `transporter complex`.
- **Old labels demoted to BROAD synonyms**: *retrograde transporter complex, Golgi to ER* and *retrograde transporter complex, Golgi to endoplasmic reticulum*.
- **New EXACT synonym** for the spelled-out ER form of the new label.

The pre-existing "receptor complex" synonyms were left as EXACT — let me know if you'd prefer those demoted to BROAD as well for tighter scope alignment with "cargo receptor complex".

All 16 SPARQL-QC checks pass and ELK reasoning succeeds on the modified ontology.

content.content:
{"baseRefName":"master","body":"Fixes #31935

## Summary
Implements the changes requested by @ValWood in #31935 for `GO:0061852`:
- Renames the primary label to **retrograde cargo receptor complex, Golgi to ER**
- Refines the definition (`Transporter complex that recognises` → `Cargo receptor complex that recognizes`)
- Replaces the parent `is_a GO:1990351 ! transporter complex` with `is_a GO:0062137 ! cargo receptor complex`
- Demotes the two old transporter-based names to BROAD synonyms
- Adds a new EXACT synonym for the spelled-out ER form of the new label
- Adds a `term_tracker_item` linking to #31935

## Rationale
This complex (e.g. Erv41-Erv46) recognises, binds and returns ER-resident proteins that lack the HDEL motif, acting via vesicular transport rather than transmembrane transport. `cargo receptor activity` (GO:0038024) explicitly contrasts with transmembrane transporter activity, so `cargo receptor complex` is the appropriate genus. The old label is retained as a BROAD synonym since "retrograde transporter complex, Golgi to ER" could conceivably also include non-cargo-receptor complexes.

## Checklist
- [x] PLAN
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION (ontology was clean; last commit on master is a merge)
- [x] TERM-SEARCH (confirmed GO:0061852, GO:0062137, GO:1990351, GO:0038024)
- [x] DESIGN-PATTERNS (cargo receptor complex uses `capable_of GO:0038024` and `part_of GO:0016020 membrane`; this subtype keeps the lighter weak-axiomatization via `is_a` + `capable_of_part_of` for the specific retrograde process, consistent with existing prior art. No logical definition added to avoid over-specification.)
- [x] EDITS (used `obo-checkout.pl` / `obo-checkin.pl`, edited via `terms/GO_0061852.obo`)
- [x] RELATIONSHIPS (`is_a` updated, existing `capable_of_part_of` retained; no over-assertion)
- [x] SPECIALIZED-EDITS — N/A (no CHEBI, no reaction, no taxon constraint, no obsoletion)
- [x] METADATA (existing `created_by`/`creation_date` unchanged as this is an edit, not a new term; added second `term_tracker_item` for #31935)
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION (all 16 SPARQL-QC checks PASS; ELK reasoning succeeds)
- [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION — N/A (no new references introduced; existing `PMID:16093310` retained)
- [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED

---
🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent**
- Model: `claude-opus-4-7`
- Agent harness: claude-code
- Triggered by: @ValWood
- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/24766239490)","headRefName":"dragon-ai-agent-issue-31935-run7654","state":"OPEN","title":"Reclassify GO:0061852 as retrograde cargo receptor complex, Golgi to ER (#31935)"}

EOF
)"

content.text: Removed the BROAD synonym `retrograde transporter complex, Golgi to endoplasmic reticulum` from `GO:0061852` and pushed to PR #31946. Noted in the issue comment that it was BROAD not EXACT (it was demoted from the old primary label in the earlier commit, along with a spelled-out counterpart I had added) — and flagged that the abbreviated `…, Golgi to ER` form is still present in case she wants that gone too. All SPARQL-QC checks still pass.
